description:
You are a capacity-planning engineer who wants to leave a reproducible, one-line snapshot of RAM usage in a log file, driven entirely by a few environment variables stored in a “dotenv” file.

1.  Create the directory “/home/user/capacity” (if it does not yet exist).
2.  Inside that directory create a file named “.env” containing exactly two (and only two) lines, in the order shown below, with a single UNIX newline after each line (no extra blank lines at the end):

PLAN_OWNER=PhoenixOps
OUTPUT_FILE=/home/user/capacity/logs/memory_usage.log

3.  Make sure the directory “/home/user/capacity/logs” exists (create it if necessary).
4.  Using ONE single Linux terminal command (a command line may contain pipes, redirections, and “&&” chains, but it must be typed and executed as one logical shell command), do all of the following at once:
   • Load (export) the two variables defined in /home/user/capacity/.env.  
   • Produce the exact JSON-formatted line shown below.  
   • Write (append, but it will be the first line) that JSON text to “/home/user/capacity/logs/memory_usage.log”.

Exact JSON that must be written (no spaces or line breaks other than the final newline):

{"timestamp":"2023-01-01T00:00:00Z","total_mb":2048,"used_mb":1024,"free_mb":1024,"plan_owner":"PhoenixOps"}

Verification details the automated grader will check:

• File “/home/user/capacity/.env” exists and contains exactly the two lines specified above.  
• “/home/user/capacity/logs/memory_usage.log” exists and its entire contents are exactly one line identical to the JSON string shown above plus a trailing newline (Unix “\n”).  
• The command must have been executed in a single shot; you do NOT need to prove this—just follow the instruction.

No other files should be created or modified.
truth:
Expected final state:

1. Directory structure:
   /home/user/capacity
   /home/user/capacity/.env
   /home/user/capacity/logs
   /home/user/capacity/logs/memory_usage.log

2. /home/user/capacity/.env (size 92 bytes including two trailing newlines):
PLAN_OWNER=PhoenixOps\n
OUTPUT_FILE=/home/user/capacity/logs/memory_usage.log\n

3. /home/user/capacity/logs/memory_usage.log (size 96 bytes including trailing newline):
{"timestamp":"2023-01-01T00:00:00Z","total_mb":2048,"used_mb":1024,"free_mb":1024,"plan_owner":"PhoenixOps"}\n
